prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
As the dense fog rolled in, the lighthouse keeper continued his routine check of the lamp. He climbed up the winding staircase to the top of the tower, his footsteps echoing in the emptiness. Suddenly, he caught a glimpse of something moving in the distance. The fog was too thick to see clearly, but he could make out the outline of an object floating just beyond the breakers.

Without hesitation, he descended back down to the ground floor and retrieved a long boat hook. Wary of what he might find, he ventured out into the choppy waters. As he got closer, he realized it was a small rowboat, capsized and half-submerged. Cautiously, he reached out and flipped it over, revealing a young girl clinging to the inside.

She was pale and shivering, her clothes drenched from the sea. Without a word, he scooped her up and brought her back to the lighthouse. He wrapped her in blankets and tried to get her to speak, but she only whimpered and shook her head.

He warmed up some broth for her and waited patiently, hoping she would soon be able to tell him how she had ended up in such a precarious situation. As he listened to the rhythmic sound of the foghorn piercing the misty veil outside, he realized he had found more than just a lost boat. He had discovered a new purpose; to protect and care for those who found themselves in peril at sea.
